# Sample Session Transcript (Test Fixture)

## Session: test-fixture-01

[User]: I've been thinking about the three-tier model. The key insight is that
community can't meaningfully exist if the user layer is incoherent. It's the
same recursion principle operating at every scale.

[Assistant]: Exactly. The pattern is: stabilization inward enables extension
outward. The household layer is structurally the build layer — features,
rules, and architecture all live here.

[User]: Right. And we decided the wiki consolidation should be a separate agent,
not bundled into the nightly retro. The retro is about surfacing signal, not
maintaining the wiki.

[Assistant]: Good separation of concerns. The nightly retro extracts, the wiki
agent synthesizes. Two distinct passes.

[User]: One open question: what's the right cadence for wiki consolidation?
Daily feels too frequent. Weekly might let drift accumulate.

[Assistant]: Worth defining a trigger — run when N new digests have landed in
retro/, or when a concept appears across more than M sessions. Event-driven
rather than time-driven.

[User]: The "Cache" concept is becoming the most densely connected node in the
graph. That density of engagement means it's emergent, not imposed.

[Assistant]: Centrality as a signal of real conceptual weight. The graph is
revealing structure rather than creating it.
